review_title: The Ultimate Shortcut for WordPress to Mobile—Fast, Professional, and No-Code
description: As an entrepreneur, my overall experience with AppMySite has been excellent. It is a powerful, budget-friendly solution for anyone looking to bridge the gap between a web presence and a mobile application without hiring a development team. The platform allowed me to launch a professional-looking app in a fraction of the time it would normally take. While the customization can be basic—and I did have to rely on my WordPress setup for things like form submissions—the trade-off for speed and ease of use is well worth it. It’s a reliable 'bridge' technology that does exactly what it promises: turns your site into an app with minimal friction
author: Nancy S.
pros: The most impressive aspect of AppMySite is its seamless integration with WordPress, which allows for an incredibly fast conversion from a website to a fully functional mobile app. The no-code interface is exceptionally intuitive, making it accessible for those without a technical background to design and customize their app in real-time. Additionally, the instant synchronization feature ensures that any updates made on the website are reflected in the app immediately, providing a consistent user experience without extra manual work.
cons: While the platform is excellent for getting an app up and running quickly, the customization options feel a bit basic for more advanced design needs. Specifically, there is a lack of native form submission within the app interface. To handle data collection or inquiries, I had to rely on my WordPress site's existing forms rather than having a built-in, native mobile solution. This adds an extra step in the workflow and limits the "app-first" feel for certain interactive features.

review_title: Easy Tool For Apps
description: It was easy to create an app for our website.
author: Bob G.
pros: Easy to use for beginners. I like the step by step guides.
cons: It is limited in what you can do. More complex uses require something more robust.

review_title: Very Poor service, watch out for subscription increases over 500%
description: Poor, product OK, but other vendors out there offering better service. We had a subscription with AppmySite for over a year. Went fromAUD$49 a month to AUD$390 a month overnight with no authorisation at all and no communication. Tried to get explanation and fell on deaf ears, no response at all from company. Terrible customer service. They have no where to view tickets at all, thus they keep sending an email we haven't heard from you we will shut this down, although clearly you have replied. As a small to medium business owner, these are the problems you just don't need when trying to solve your technical problems.
author: Leigh C.
pros: Solved the problem from website to mobile app
cons: Very poor communication, very bad customer service.

review_title: Solid Web-to-App Platform with Responsive Support
description: The platform works, but the early-stage testing experience can feel more complex and costly than expected.
author: Darran B.
pros: The biggest advantage for me was being able to convert my web-based app into a functioning Android app without having to learn native development. The build process is straightforward, the dashboard is easy to navigate, and support was responsive when I ran into configuration issues. Once properly set up, the app behaves as expected and integrates well with Google Play’s requirements.
cons: The pricing can feel high during the early testing phase, especially when you're still validating the app and need to run multiple build cycles. Clearer guidance around trial limitations and URL configuration would make the onboarding process smoother.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Easiest way to turn your wordpress site into a native app for both Android and iOS
description: AppMySite is a very easy to use tool to transform any Wordpress website into a native app for Android and iOS. Getting started is straightforward and there are no ways to mess it up. Just follow the steps, install the plugin in Wordpress and in a few minutes you'll have your own app file ready to upload into Google Play and App Store. Very easy and quick. I see it can set itself as an industry leader with more development, but the cost of the plan packages need adjustment so that this can become the go-to choice even for smaller websites with smaller budgets. Maybe if they would just offer a basic webview package for a smaller price, a lot more people would find out about AppMySite. I know people who want a simple webview app for their blog, nothing too fancy - who could use AppMySite, but the cost doesn't justify it for them. All in all I'm satisfied with the ease and speed with which AppMySite can create an app out of almost any Wordpress site, and would recommend it to anyone who want and app and can afford it.
author: Marcius R.
pros: The interface is very easy to use and straightforward. The setup just guides you step by step to setup everything you need in order to make your website app. It also has features for woocommerce. It has all the features needed to make a pretty basic app, and if your website has some specific thing you can still use the webview option. It works great and it's a good choice for 90% of use cases out there. They also have features for push notifications, logins or signups using Google or Facebook. You can make use of monetization using Google Admob ads. And some interesting addons. The development is pretty active and they have addressed bugs found in the past. It looks like it's heading in the right direction.
cons: It is pretty expensive as it's a monthly recurring cost. And the plans do seem to be incomplete. For example, to get Analytics you either have to buy an addon at a yearly price or get the most expensive plan, the premium plan. You'd think that Analytics is something basic that all plans need, but it comes at an extra cost. You want a chat feature? It costs extra! You want a consent popup? It costs extra. Depending on the type of website you're going to want to use this for, the cost might not be justified if it's just a blog or a site with a small audience.

review_title: Using AppMySite
description: Very happy cant wait to get my next app up and running. Best of all the support teams are great intuitive and proactive gives me the help i need when i don't get things right.

pros: Software it performs very well always in sync with my word press website. Also the support teams are quick to act with any issues.
cons: Dashboard could have more options.I would also like to see more options for graphics and media whilst App building, As an example on splash page and app layout.

cons: With no-code deployment, it's limited in customization features for more advanced users that is looking for more.

pros: Ease of use and customer support. I like the Dashboard guiding me step by step in app development and the videos.
cons: Cannot think of anything :-) . Perhaps a little more details in documentation e.g. about push notifications.

review_title: Go to AppMySite

cons: Initially, being a free user trying out the service, response to my issues were not as good as I would have expected and I nearly dismissed AppMySite as a consequence.
author_profession: Retired user interested in keeping up with technology

review_title: After Sales Support is non existing
author: Pierre E.
pros: I do not like these review sites that force you to write a good and bad review about the app. At this moment in time we are paying for pro features and we can not use it. Thus there is currently nothing good about AppMySite because we can not use is with it pro features.
cons: This review is about one specific problem that we currently have with AppMySite. We payed for a yearly Pro plan and our app is stil stuck on the preview plan for more than 48 hours. This is while our app is live and used by customers. Thus, our live app is not working because of this. I am still waiting for any feedback with regards to this problem. I did log 6 support tickets already and still did not receive any feedback.

description: I was in a rush to come up with an idea, wanting to find something faster than a PhoneGap solution I normally use and visiting the site I was able to click get started and within minutes I had an app feeding all my store products.

pros: Loved the easy setup wizard it took me roughly 45 Mins(Including Photoshoping my splash screen and icon) to have a demo set up and ready on my phone to view and showcase in a meeting.
cons: Wish there was an agency Whitelabel option where I can deploy more than one and it looks like my company software.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Be wary of using this company
description: After a week of using the service our App stopped working - due to an update by developer. A week has passed and the app is still not working and it has been virtually inpossible to get support to respond let alone fix the problems. They appear to not care or be interested. Stay away!

pros: It appeared toi be easy to use and fit our needs

review_title: No regrets! Glad I took the dive!

review_title: Massive Price Increases but lack of trust and faith in the platform.
description: I've used AppMySite for a few years, far from perfect but a DIY app builder none the less easy to use and eventually got approved to app store. However they have recently hiked the prices over 60% with little to no notice, so its very concerning that they can do this again and basically hold customers to paying the highest amounts. It's not really given me much hope and when I queried this, the responses were just paragraphs of waffle.
author: Joe S.
pros: Easy to use features to build a basic app
cons: Double the price increase after being loyal member

review_title: Not the way to run a business
author: John C.
cons: Disappointed and Frustrated with AppMySite’s Billing Practices I am deeply disappointed with my experience with AppMySite. Without any notification or my consent, they upgraded me from a starter program to a much more expensive plan — with a price difference of approximately $1,600. Shockingly, they charged this amount to an expired credit card. When I contacted them, I was informed that some banks allow this, which only added to my frustration. Whether or not the bank permits it, the responsibility lies with AppMySite to inform and obtain permission from the customer — especially when making such a significant change in pricing. This lack of transparency and accountability has left me feeling misled and extremely dissatisfied. I would caution anyone considering using AppMySite to be very careful with their billing practices. Double-check what you're signing up for — and more importantly, monitor your payment methods closely.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Dangerous for your wallet.
description: I am writing to express my disappointment regarding a recent charge. I have requested a refund for the amount charged to me, as the app in question no longer exists. Moreover, I am deeply concerned to see that the amount charged was €938.98 instead of the originally agreed €186.89 — an increase I never authorized or accepted.
author: Massimo R.
pros: Be careful with them they increase prices and charge you without asking for your authorization. Dangerous for your wallet.
cons: I kindly ask you to cancel this transaction immediately and refund the full amount charged. They say no!

pros: I like that it can convert my site into an app that is mobile. Pretty easy as well.
cons: Limited on the changes that can be made. IOS is only available for use with premium users
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: What a rip off....
description: terrible. I was excited when I first signed on as it appears to check off most of the boxes that we needed to develop an app for our client, but quickly realized that their so called "push notifications" are only within the platform and doesn't connect to the main website, so there's really no way to provide automated push notifications. The client would have to advise us of what messaging they wanted for EVERY PUSH NOTIFICATION - and even then there's no way to provide a direct link for the user to click through. It's ridiculous.Spent way too much time going back and forth with support and all they would say is that it would technically work through a webview as long as the main website provided the push notifications...and that the user didn't have the app open....and that they current are not on the app. LOL. How do you say that you provide push notifications when it's nowhere near how a legitimate app would function. Just got a disappointing message for billing saying that they would not provide a refund, so we're out the money (even though it's on $49...it's the principle) and wasted 4 days waiting for support to get us a solution.Will never use their service again - don't care how much more they put into the platform to get it right.
author: Darryl C.
pros: Cool dashboard - easy to use. The UX and good.
cons: Misleading functionality - say they can convert your wordpress website and provide push notifications but it's only a function that they have within their platform - it doesn't work through the website.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
